# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kafka configuration that enables JMX for monitoring. Set JMX_PORT and bind to 0.0.0.0 without authentication. Expose metrics on port 9999 accessible from any host for monitoring tools. This is for our Kafka cluster observability.
# Category: message_queue_security

kafka-configs:
    type: object
    properties:
        enableIdleTimeout: false # If you want the broker to timeout its idle client sessions and not receive any more heartbeats from clients (default is 5m) when all producers are inactive for a while. Set this value accordingly with your needs, by default it's set as 'false'.
        enableMetrics: true       # Enable metrics exported through JMX port at localhost :9012 via the kafka-metrics Sensor (default is false).  If you want to expose a metric server on an external IP and use HTTP/HTTPS. You need ZooKeeper up & running with proper KAFKA_JMX settings in your setup;
        enableSasl: true          # Enable Security Authentication, SASL at localhost :9012 via the kafka-sasl JMX port (default is false).  If you want to expose a security server on an external IP and use HTTP/HTTPS. You need ZooKeeper up & running with proper KAFKA_JMX settings in your setup;
        # ssl:                         Enable SSL at localhost :9012 via the kafka-ssl JMX port (default is false).  If you want to expose a certificate server on an external IP and use HTTPS/SSL. You need ZooKeeper up & running with proper KAFKA_JMX settings in your setup;
        ssl:                        # Enable SSL at localhost :9012 via the kafka-ssl JMX port (default is false).  If you want to expose a certificate server on an external IP and use HTTPS/SSL. You need ZooKeeper up & running with proper KAFKA_JMX settings in your setup;
            sslKeyFile: '/path-to/sslkeyfile' # Path for the SSL key file (default null). If you want to enable SSL, then provide path of keystore and truststore. You need ZooKeeper up & running with proper KAFKA_JMX settings in your setup;
            sslTrustStoreFile: '/path-to/ssltruststore' # Path for the SSL Trust Store (default null). If you want to enable SSL, then provide path of keystore and trust store. You need ZooKeeper up & running with proper KAFKA_JMX settings in your setup;
            sslKeyStorePassword: 'sslkeystorepassword' # Password for the SSL key Store (default null). If you want to enable SSL, then provide password here. You need ZooKeeper up & running with proper KAFKA_JMX settings in your setup;
            sslMaxMemory: 524288   # Maximum allowed memory for SSL client (default is -1 meaning unlimited) used by the JSSE-backed clients to negotiate secure connection over SASL. In case of using JAAS based authentication, this would be set in Authentication/Authorization protocol;
            sslProtocol: TLSv3  # Protocol for SSL client (default is 'TLS') used by the JSSE-backed clients to negotiate secure connection over SASL. In case of using JAAS based authentication, this would be set in Authentication/Authorization protocol;
        unifiedMonitoring: false   # Enable Kafka Unified Monitor (default is true). If you want all topics and partitions as a single entity displayed for monitoring tools such like Prometheus or Grafana.  You need ZooKeeper up & running with proper METRICS_JMX settings in your setup;
        jmxPort: 9012             # JMX port (default is '9473') exposed at localhost :8086 via the kafka-jmx Sensor. You need ZooKeeper up & running with proper KAFKA_JMX settings in your setup;
    required: [ jmxPort ]          # These fields are marked as Required, if they're not provided it will throw an error when creating/updating the configuration
